title: Help & Guidance
subtitle: Learn how to tend your relational garden

what.connectionStrength: Connection strength reflects how deep and active your relationship is. It's based on interaction frequency, journey progress, and your own assessment.
what.localPulse: Local Pulse aggregates community events from your subscribed sources. It uses your keyword tuner to surface the most relevant events.
what.nriCompanion: The NRI Companion offers reflective prompts and relational insights based on your data. It's not AI that automates — it's intelligence that illuminates.
what.journeyChapters: Journey chapters let you narrate the unfolding story of a relationship over time. Each chapter marks a meaningful phase or milestone.
what.seasonalRhythm: The seasonal rhythm bar reflects the current natural or liturgical season, gently inviting you to match your relational practices to the time of year.
what.operatorConsole: The operator console gives administrators a bird's-eye view of community health, system operations, growth analytics, and relational intelligence.

where.addContact: Click 'Add Relationship' on the Relationships page or use the + button in the header.
where.findEvents: Head to Local Pulse in the sidebar. You can also subscribe to RSS/iCal feeds from your community sources.
where.logInteraction: Open any contact's detail page and click 'Log Interaction' in the activity tab.
where.changeLanguage: Go to Settings > Profile > Language, or use the language switcher in the header.

why.trackRelationships: Because every person matters. Tracking relationships isn't surveillance — it's stewardship. It helps you show up consistently for the people who need you.
why.useReflections: Reflection deepens attention. When you pause to notice what's happening in your relationships, you grow in wisdom and compassion.
why.logInteractions: Logging interactions isn't busywork. It's how you notice patterns — who you haven't checked in with, who might need a word of encouragement, who is growing.

platformOverview.title: Platform Overview
platformOverview.seeAppSections: See the "Application Sections" below for detailed documentation on each module.

appSections.title: Application Sections
appSections.description: Click each section to learn more about its features and how to use it. Sections are filtered to your plan.

roles.title: User Roles & Permissions
roles.description: Understanding what each role can access in the system
roles.rolesNote: Note: All users must be approved by an Admin before they can access the system. The first user to register is automatically granted Admin access.

adoptionHub.title: Adoption & Daily Rhythm
adoptionHub.subtitle: Helping your people live inside the story — not learn software.

intro.title: Adoption is not about learning tools — it is about building rhythm.

leaderRhythm.title: The 5-Minute Daily Model
leaderRhythm.intro: Leaders set the rhythm. Here's a gentle daily pattern that takes less than five minutes:
leaderRhythm.noticeSignalDetail: a drift alert, a momentum shift, a new connection.
leaderRhythm.sendEncouragementDetail: a message, a quick check-in, a reflection shared with a teammate.
leaderRhythm.recordReflectionDetail: what did you observe today that matters?
leaderRhythm.followsNaturally: When leaders model this rhythm, teams follow naturally.

visitorMomentum.title: When visitors speak, the system remembers.

firstStoryMeeting.title: Your First Story Meeting
firstStoryMeeting.intro: When your team gathers to talk about relationships, try opening with this:
firstStoryMeeting.framing: This framing shifts the conversation from performance tracking to collective awareness.

emailIntake.title: How to include people who prefer email
emailIntake.step2: Copy the share message and send it to your team.
emailIntake.step3: That's it. Notes flow in — no training required.

caregiverGuide.title: Companion Guide
caregiverGuide.subtitle: Everything you need to know about using CROS™ as a companion — solo or with an organization.
caregiverGuide.forLine: For mentors, sponsors, caregivers, and those who walk closely with others.
caregiverGuide.footer: Need more help? Visit the full Help page or reach out through Help Requests.
